title: Advanced Grammar Deep Study
description: Advanced concepts including Voice, Narration, Conditionals, and Complex Tense Relations.

rules: When two actions happen in the past, the earlier action uses Past Perfect, and the later action uses Past Indefinite.
For interrupted actions, the ongoing action uses Past Continuous, and the interrupting action uses Past Indefinite.

examples.english: The thief had escaped before the police arrived.
examples.hindi: पुलिस के आने से पहले चोर भाग चुका था।

examples.english: I was sleeping when he came.
examples.hindi: मैं सो रहा था जब वह आया।

rules: Focuses on the action rather than the subject performing the action.
Object of active sentence becomes subject of passive sentence. Uses 'to be' verb + Past Participle (V3).

examples.english: A letter was written by her.
examples.hindi: उसके द्वारा एक पत्र लिखा गया।

rules: Direct speech quotes the exact words spoken. Indirect speech reports what was said without exact quotes.
Tenses typically shift back one step in the past when reporting in the past tense.

examples.english: He said that he was going to the market.
examples.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह बाजार जा रहा था।

rules: Zero Conditional: General truths (If + Present Simple, Present Simple).
First Conditional: Real future possibilities (If + Present Simple, will + base verb).

examples.english: If it rains, we will stay at home.
examples.hindi: अगर बारिश होती है, तो हम घर पर रहेंगे।
